order-by.label: ORDER BY Clause
order-by.summary: How to sort query results by one or more columns.

sk1.title: What is ORDER BY?
sk1.bodyMarkdown: The `ORDER BY` clause in SQL is used to sort the result set of a query by one or more columns. By default, sorting is in ascending order, but you can specify descending order as well. This helps you organize your data in a meaningful way, such as alphabetically, numerically, or by date.

sk2.title: Syntax of ORDER BY
sk2.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for using `ORDER BY` is:

```sql
SELECT column1, column2
FROM table_name
ORDER BY column1 [ASC|DESC], column2 [ASC|DESC];
```
- `ASC` sorts in ascending order (default).
- `DESC` sorts in descending order.
You can sort by multiple columns by separating them with commas.

sk3.title: Example: Sorting Products by Price
sk3.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you have a `products` table. To list all products from lowest to highest price:

```sql
SELECT name, price
FROM products
ORDER BY price ASC;
```
To list from highest to lowest price:

```sql
SELECT name, price
FROM products
ORDER BY price DESC;
```

q1.title: What does the ORDER BY clause do in SQL?

options.a: It filters rows based on a condition.
options.b: It sorts the result set by one or more columns.
options.c: It groups rows that have the same values.
options.d: It limits the number of rows returned.

q2.title: Which of the following are true about ORDER BY?

options.a: You can sort by multiple columns.
options.b: The default sort order is ascending.
options.c: ORDER BY must always be used with GROUP BY.
options.d: You can specify descending order with DESC.

q3.title: Arrange the SQL keywords to form a valid ORDER BY statement.

q4.title: Sort Students by Last Name
q4.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the `students` table and sort the results by the `last_name` column in ascending order.
